title: Duck Pond Energy Conservation Simulator
description: Michelle stands at the edge of a circular pond and tosses rubber ducks into the water. Each duck carries kinetic energy that converts to wave energy on impact, obeying conservation of energy. The user controls toss angle, power, and spin to land ducks on floating targets while managing a finite energy budget with full undo/redo support.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 with GLTFLoader and OrbitControls from CDN. Load Michelle.glb positioned at the edge of a circular pond (a reflective blue disc with subtle animated vertex displacement for waves). Load Duck.glb as a template that gets cloned for each toss. Scene setup: ground plane with grass texture, ambient light 0.4 intensity, directional light casting shadows, hemisphere light for sky fill. Camera starts at a 45-degree elevated angle behind Michelle looking toward the pond center. Place 5 floating target rings on the pond surface at varying distances. Implement a finite energy budget of 100 units displayed as a top-bar gauge. Interactions: (1) Click-drag on a power meter slider (0-100%) to set toss force. (2) Use left/right arrow keys to rotate Michelle's facing direction in 15-degree increments with her animation blending. (3) Press up/down arrows to adjust toss arc angle between 20-70 degrees. (4) Press spacebar to execute the toss, triggering Michelle's throw animation. The duck launches as a projectile following parabolic trajectory with gravity. (5) On water impact, subtract kinetic energy (0.5*m*v²) from the budget and spawn concentric wave rings that propagate outward and interact with other wave rings additively. (6) Ducks float and bob on the surface after landing. (7) If a duck lands within a target ring, the ring glows green and awards bonus energy back to the budget (conservation reward loop). (8) Implement Ctrl+Z for undo that reverses the last toss, removes the duck, restores energy, and resets Michelle's orientation. (9) Implement Ctrl+Y for redo. (10) A reset button restores the full initial state. Maintain an action history stack for undo/redo. Display current energy budget, ducks tossed count, and targets hit. All energy transfers must visually show via animated number popups near the impact point. The total energy in the system (budget + kinetic + wave) must remain constant, enforcing conservation law.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  energyBudget: number,         // remaining energy units (0-100)
  tossPower: number,         // current power slider value (0-100)
  tossAngle: number,         // arc angle in degrees (20-70)
  michelleFacing: number,         // rotation angle in degrees (0-360)
  ducksLaunched: number,         // total count of ducks thrown
  ducksOnTarget: number,         // count of ducks that hit targets
  activeProjectiles: number,         // ducks currently in flight
  waveRingCount: number,         // total wave rings spawned
  lastImpactEnergy: number,         // kinetic energy of most recent impact
  simulationActive: boolean,         // whether simulation is running
}
